title: Anaergia Launches C$25M Biomethane Projects in Spain Turning Olive Waste into Cleaner Fuel
content: Anaergia Inc., through its subsidiary Anaergia S.r.l., has initiated two new anaerobic digestion projects in Andujar and Arjona, Spain, valued at approximately C$25 million. These contracts are part of a broader C$184 million agreement announced in August 2025 with D.B. Andalucia to develop over 15 facilities. The plants will utilize Anaergiaâ€™s technology to convert agricultural waste, specifically olive pomace, into biomethane, a cleaner fuel alternative. Each facility is designed to process 100,000 tonnes of waste annually and is expected to be completed within 18 months. This initiative underscores the companies' commitment to the energy transition, turning a byproduct of olive oil production into a sustainable energy source.
author: Analysts Account
